name: Paul Whiteman
description: American jazz musician and popular bandleader (1890–1967)
abstract: Paul Samuel Whiteman (March 28, 1890 – December 29, 1967) was an American Jazz bandleader, composer, orchestral director, and violinist. As the leader of one of the most popular dance bands in the United States during the 1920s and early 1930s, Whiteman produced recordings that were immensely successful, and press notices often referred …

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: the same way that Paul Whiteman – like you hear Eubie Blake talk about it. He said, “Paul Whiteman is not the King of Jazz. He is not the King of Jazz. James Reese Europe was the King of Jazz.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Havens
about_quotes.evidence: I would say mostly it was recordings of large orchestras, such as Paul Whiteman... I became acquainted with the dance orchestras of the time by just playing these records.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Havens interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: They had a lot of Bert Williams records and Paul Whiteman records. ... I used to lay under our old Majestic radio and listen to Duke Ellington and Paul Whiteman

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gallodoro
about_quotes.evidence: Whiteman heard me play... he made me on offer and I accepted it... I was with Whiteman all these years. We toured the whole country from coast to coast.

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Paul Whiteman is mentioned by the interviewer in a historical comparison; the interviewee does not respond or provide any personal interaction.

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Don, back in 1925 and '26, had wrote worked for Jean Goldkette's Gene Galkit's orchestra and Paul Whiteman
